title: Repository Purpose, Risk Profile, and Definition of Done · Risk Brief: Done Contract
summary: Design quality gates for a new repository according to its actual failure costs. Planned artifact: Add and verify the gates done contract increment in a repository engineering contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Define machine-verifiable and human-reviewed completion criteria.
Rank correctness, security, accessibility, performance, compatibility, and maintenance risks.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# Risk-profiled repository gate portfolio
# Each gate needs purpose, command, failure fixture, owner, and suppression policy.

steps.title: Predict Done Contract
steps.instruction:
Use a changed theory case derived from this module: gates risk contract. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

Use a changed theory case derived from this module: gates risk contract. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. Before reading, predict which action preserves the Done Contract contract.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for define machine-verifiable and human-reviewed completion criteria.

content.text: Define machine-verifiable and human-reviewed completion criteria. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Check preconditions, perform the operation in an inspectable order, then verify the result and a boundary case.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case derived from this module: gates risk contract. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Done Contract, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a repository engineering contract.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: A passing build is a complete definition of done. This fails the Done Contract contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

steps.title: Build Done Contract evidence
steps.instruction: Append a gates-done-contract-gro6p: manifest entry with concrete verify plus failure, owner, or repair evidence.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Done Contract into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
gates-done-contract-gro6p:
  verify: [replace with a deterministic check]
  failure: [replace with the rejected condition]
  repair: [replace with a local reproduction and correction path]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

steps.title: Defend the Done Contract model
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Done Contract applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Define machine-verifiable and human-reviewed completion criteria.
